item.name: Drow Poison (TAF)
entries: {@i Injure:} A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution save or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is also {@condition unconscious} while {@condition poisoned} in this way. An {@condition unconscious} creature loses this condition if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it.

item.name: Toxic Vial (TAF)
entries: {@i Inhaled:} A creature hit by this poison and each creature within 5 feet must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution save. On a failed save it takes {@damage 2d8} poison damage and becomes {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute, on a successful save it takes only half damage. A {@condition poisoned} creature must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Truth Serum (TAF)
entries: {@i Ingested:} A creature hit by this poison must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour. The {@condition poisoned} creature can't knowingly speak a lie, as if under the effect of a {@spell zone of truth} spell.

item.name: Ethereal Agony (TAF)
entries: {@i Inhaled:} A creature hit by this poison and each creature within 5 feet of it takes {@damage 2d6} poison damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. At the start of each of its turns, a {@condition poisoned} creature takes {@damage 1d6} poison damage and must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 14) ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Grung Touch (TAF)
entries: {@i Injury:} For 1 minute, a creature subjected to this poison must roll {@dice 1d4} at the start of its turn to determine the effect it suffers. At the end of its turn, the target must make a Wisdom save (DC 14), ending the effect early on a success.

rows: The target is {@condition poisoned} and must scream loud, while it screams it can't cast spells with verbal components.
The target is {@condition poisoned} and can move only by climbing, if it has a flying speed it can't take actions or reactions until it lands.
The target is {@condition poisoned} and must soak itself in liquid, it must use its turn to move into a body of water or do nothing in its turn.
The creature is {@condition poisoned} and must use its action to eat the nearest food.

item.name: Torpor (TAF)
entries: {@i Ingested:} A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a Intelligence saving throw (DC 14) or become {@condition poisoned} for {@dice 2d4} hours. While {@condition poisoned} in this way a creature is also {@condition unconscious}.

item.name: Hallucinating Spore (TAF)
entries: {@i Inhaled:} A creature hit by this poison and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a Wisdom save (DC 16). On a failed save it takes {@damage 3d10} psychic damage and becomes {@condition blinded} for 10 minutes, on a successful save it only takes half that damage. A creature {@condition blinded} in this way must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Midnight Tears (TAF)
entries: {@i Ingested:} A creature that ingests this poison suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight. If the poison has not been neutralized before then, the creature must succeed on a Constitution save (DC 16), taking {@damage 10d6} acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success.

item.name: Wild Magic Poison (TAF)
entries: {@i Injure:} A creature subjected to this poison suffers one of the effects described in the following table (roll {@dice 1d4} to determine the effect).

rows: It must succeed on a Constitution save (DC 16). It takes {@damage 5d10} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful save.
It must succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 16) or use its turn to move within 5 feet of its nearest ally and make an attack against it.

It must succeed on a Constitution save (DC 16). On a failed save it is {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save it is {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn.
The target takes {@damage 3d10} lightning damage and must succeed on a Constitution save (DC 16) or become {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Yugoloth Blood (TAF)
entries: {@i Injure:} A creature hit by this poison must succeed on a Constitution save (DC 16). On a failed save, it takes {@damage 5d8} necrotic damage and is {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute, on a successful save it only takes half that damage. It must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Avernus' Kiss (TAF)
entries: {@i Injure:} A creature subjected to this poison takes {@damage 6d6} fire damage. For 1 minute, at the start of its turn, the creature takes {@damage 6d6} fire damage and must make a Constitution save (DC 18) ending this effect on a success.

item.name: Black Dragon Breath (TAF)
entries: {@i Inhaled:} A creature hit by this poison and each creature within 10 feet of it, must succeed on a Constitution save DC 18 taking {@damage 10d8} acid damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

item.name: Lolth Bite (TAF)
entries: {@i Injure:} A creature subjected to this poison takes {@damage 8d8} necrotic damage and must make Wisdom save (DC 18), or become {@condition incapacitated} and have its speed reduced to 0 for 1 hour. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the saving throw ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Blade of Immolation
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, and on a hit, it deals an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage. When you score a critical hit using this weapon, the target also has the {@condition restrained} condition until the end of its next turn, as fiery bands lash around it.

{@b Invoking the Rune.} When you hit with this weapon, you can use your reaction to invoke its rune. Doing so increases the extra fire damage dealt to {@damage 2d6}. Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.

item.name: Bow of Conflagration
entries: Ammunition fired from this bow blazes brightly. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. When you hit using this weapon, the target and each creature of your choice within 5 feet of it takes {@damage 1d6} force damage.

item.name: Bow of Melodies
entries: This bow has multiple strings and resembles a lyre or small harp. If you're proficient in Performance, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. This bonus increases to +2 if you have expertise in Performance. When you hit using this weapon, the target takes extra thunder damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

item.name: Divine Touched Weapon
entries: This weapon has been touched by divine power, blessed by a saint or wielded by a holy hero.

{@b Slumbering (Uncommon).} Once every turn, when you hit with this weapon, you can choose a creature other than you within 30 feet of you and grant it temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.
{@b Stirring (Rare).} You gain a +1 on attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. Once every turn, when you hit with this weapon, you can cause one of the following effects.

items: {@b Holy Strike.} The target of the attack takes {@damage 1d12} extra radiant damage.
{@b Holy Heal.} You can choose a creature other than you within 30 feet of you and grant it temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d12} + your proficiency bonus.

entries: {@b Wakened (Very Rare).} It has and improves the Stirring property. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2. In addition, when you use either of the effects granted by the Stirring property, you can roll {@dice 2d12} instead of {@dice 1d12}.

item.name: Dragon Wrath Weapon
entries: This weapon can absorb the energy of a chromatic or metallic dragon’s breath weapon.

{@b Slumbering (Uncommon).} When you score a critical hit with this weapon, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of you takes 5 points of acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison damage. The damage dealt is the one associated with the dragon’s breath weapon.

{@b Stirring (Rare).} It has the Slumbering property. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the type chosen for the Slumbering property.

{@b Wakened (Very Rare).} It has the Slumbering property, and it improves on the Stirring property. The bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage dealt by the weapon increases to {@damage 2d6}. Also, as an action, you can unleash a 30-foot cone of destructive energy from the weapon. Each creature in that area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 8d6} damage of the type chosen for the Slumbering property on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this action is used, it can’t be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Fool's Blade
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The blade has 6 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.
{@b Fool's Feint.} You can feint to target your enemy’s weak points. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can spend 1 charge to deal {@damage 2d12} extra psychic damage.

{@b Misdirect.} When a creature within 60 feet of you targets you with an attack, you can use a reaction to spend 1 charge and force that creature to make a DC 17 Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the attack instead targets another creature of your choice that is within the attacker's reach.

item.name: Gem Infused Weapon
entries: This weapon is fashioned from the horn or claw of a gem dragon and can channel its power.

{@b Slumbering (Uncommon).} The weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges at dawn. When you hit a creature using this weapon, you can expend 1 charge to regain a number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

{@b Stirring (Rare).} It has the Slumbering property and it makes you regain {@dice 1d10} extra hit points. In addition, when you hit with an attack roll using this sword, the target takes {@damage 1d10} points of force, necrotic, psychic, radiant or thunder damage. The damage dealt is the one associated with the dragon’s breath weapon.
{@b Wakened (Very Rare).} It has and improves the Stirring property. Both the healing and extra damage of the Stirring property increase to {@dice 2d10}.

item.name: Nyx's Mirror Feather
entries: This glowing plume can be wielded like a weapon. While holding a weapon in one hand and this item in the other, unless this weapon is the product of a spell, the feather becomes a perfect replica of the weapon. If the original weapon leaves your persona, the feather returns to its original form. If the weapon has special abilities, such as being able to perform a special action or cast a spell, the Feather doesn’t mimic these features.

item.name: Nyx's Sheath of Homecoming
entries: Nyx used this sheath as a perch to take a little nap, when she left, it became so heartbroken that get abandonment issues. You can sheath a weapon with the thrown property in it. If you throw that weapon, immediately after the attack, it vanishes and reappears in the sheath.

item.name: Primal Force Weapon
entries: This weapon channels the power of nature, forged by an archfey or crafted from a divine tree.
{@b Slumbering (Uncommon).} Once every turn, when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push or pull it 5 feet from you horizontally.

{@b Stirring (Rare).} It has the Slumbering property. In addition, you gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon. When you hit with this weapon you can deal {@damage 1d4} piercing damage to each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target of the attack.

{@b Wakened (Very Rare).} It has the Slumbering property and improves the Stirring property. In addition, the bonus to attack and damage rolls increases to +2, and the damage dealt by the Stirring property increases to {@damage 2d4}.

item.name: Starshot Bow
entries: The weapon has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit using this weapon, you can spend 1 charge to activate one of the following features.
{@b Balance.} A creature of your choice within 60 feet of you can regain hit points equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your proficiency bonus.
{@b Flames.} Until the end of your next turn, when you hit using this weapon, the attack deals an additional {@damage 2d8} fire damage.
{@b Rogue.} Until the end of your next turn, you have the {@condition invisible} condition, and anything you are wearing or carrying is also invisible.

item.name: Wand of the War Mage
entries: While holding this wand, you gain a bonus to your spell attack rolls and spell save DC determined by the wand’s rarity. In addition, your spell attacks ignore Half Cover.
{@b Uncommon.} +1 bonus on your spell attack rolls and spell save DC.
{@b Rare.} +2 bonus on your spell attack rolls and spell save DC.
{@b Very Rare.} +3 bonus on your spell attack rolls and spell save DC.

weapons: martial weapons that have the Loading property
tools: one type of artisan's tools of your choice

default: Choose A or B: (A) Scale Mail, light crossbow and 20 bolts, Thieves' tools, Tools chosen for the tools proficiency above and Dungeoneer's Pack, 20 gp; (B) 150 GP

subclass.name: Drakewarden
subclassFeatures: Level 7: Bond of Fang and Scale|Ranger|XPHB|Drakewarden|AliFosche|7

classFeature.name: Magical Tinkering
entries: You know how to repair or modify mundane items. You gain the following benefits.
{@b Fixing.} You learn the {@spell Mending|xphb} cantrip and it doesn't count against the number of your Artificer's cantrips.

{@b Modifying.} You learn the {@spell Prestidigitation|xphb} cantrip and it doesn't count against the number of your Artificer's cantrips. Also, you can use an action to infuse a nonmagical object with one of the effects of Prestidigitation. Each time a certain circumstance occurs, the object casts the Prestidigitation with the chosen effect. You describe that circumstance when you use this feature. Prestidigitation remains infused into the object for up to 8 hours.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to cast spells through your tools expertise. See chapter 7 for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules with Artificer spells, which appear in the Artificer spell list later in the class description.

{@b Cantrips.} You know two cantrips of your choice from the Artificer spell list. Whenever you gain an Artificer level, you can replace one of your cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the Artificer spell list. When you reach Artificer levels 8 and 14, you learn another cantrip of your choice from the Artificer spell list, as shown in the cantrips column in the Artificer Features table.
{@b Spell Slots.} The Artificer Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long rest.

{@b Prepared Spells of Level 1+.} You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Artificer spells. The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Artificer levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Artificer Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Artificer spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Artificer Features table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Artificer, your list of prepared spells can include six Artificer spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination. If another Artificer feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Artificer spells for you.

{@b Changing your Prepared Spells.} Whenever you finish a Long rest, you can change one your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Artificer spells for which you have spell slots.
{@b Spellcasting Ability.} Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Artificer spells.
{@b Spellcasting Focus.} You can use a set of Artisan's Tools or one of your Artificer Infusions as a Spellcasting Focus for your Artificer spells.

entries.caption: Artificer Expanded Spells
rows: {@spell Circle of Power|xphb}, {@spell Mass Cure Wounds|xphb}

classFeature.name: Artificer Infusions
entries: You've learnt to infuse magic in mundane items, thus you gain the following benefits.

{@b Infusions Known.} When you gain this feature, pick two Artificer Infusions to learn. You learn additional infusions when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Infusions Known column of the Artificer Class table. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of the Artificer Infusions you learned with a new one.

{@b Artificer Infusions.} The description of each Artificer Infusion is detailed at the end of this class description. The Infusion's description details its prerequisites. You can't learn an infusion if you lack its prerequisites and can't learn an infusion more than once.

{@b Infusing an Item.} When you finish a Long rest, you can touch a nonmagical object and imbue it with one of your Artificer Infusions. When you reach certain levels in this class, you can infuse more items at a time. The number of infusions you can activate is shown in the Infused Items column of the Artificer Features table. If the item requires attunement, you can attune yourself to it the instant you infuse the item. An infusion lasts until you replace it with another, or until you die. If you infuse an object when you've already reached your Infused Items' maximum, the oldest infusion immediately ends.

classFeature.name: Artificer Restoration
entries: Working with your tools for 1 minute you restore part of your items' energy. You regain a spell slot which level is up to half the number of your Infused Items (rounded up) and you can replace one of your active infusions with another infusion you know. Once you use this feature you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long rest.

classFeature.name: Artificer Subclass
entries: You choose your Artificer subclass. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 5th, 9th, and 15th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature granted by your Artificer subclass.

classFeature.name: Tool Expertise
entries: You can add your Intelligence modifier to each ability check made to use a Tool.

classFeature.name: Flash of Genius
entries: You can come up with solutions under pressure. When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes a D20 Test, you can use your reaction to add your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) to it. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature granted by your Artificer subclass.

classFeature.name: Magic Item Adept
entries: You achieve a profound understanding of magic items. You gain the following benefits.
You can attune to up to four magic items at once.
When you craft a magic item of common or uncommon rarity, it takes you a quarter of the normal time and it costs you half as much of the usual gold.

classFeature.name: Spell-storing Item
entries: You can now store a spell in an object. When you finish a Long Rest, you can touch an item and store a spell in it, choose a level 1 or 2 spell from the Artificer spell list that requires 1 action to cast. A creature holding the object can take a magic action to cast the spell, using your spellcasting ability modifier. This object can be used a number of times equal to twice your Intelligence modifier or until you use this feature again to store a spell in another object.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Magic Item Savant
entries: Your skill with magic items deepens. You gain the following benefits.
You can attune to up to five magic items at once.
You ignore all requirements on attuning to or using a magic item.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: You gain a feature granted by your Artificer subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Magic Item Master
entries: Your bond with magic items has reached its pinnacle. You gain the following benefits.
You can attune to up to six magic items at once.
You gain a +1 bonus to all saving throws per magic item you are currently attuned to.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an Epic Boon feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Soul of the Artifice
entries: You develop a protective connection to your magic items. If you drop to 0 Hit Points and don't die outright, you can take a Reaction to end one of your Artificer Infusions and instead change your hit points to a number equal to your Artificer level.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Speech of the Woods
entries: You gain the ability to converse with beasts and many fey. You know Sylvan and have the Speak with Animals spells prepared. You can cast Speak with Animals without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Spirit Totem
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to summon an incorporeal spirit in a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The spirit manifests as a 15–foot Emanation centered on that point. As a Bonus Action, you can move the spirit up to 30 feet to a point you can see. It persists for 1 minute or until you're incapacitated. When you summon the spirit, choose one of the following effects:

entries.name: Bear Spirit.
entries: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison. Each creature of your choice within the area gains resistance to the chosen damage types.

entries.name: Hawk Spirit.
entries: When a creature makes an attack against a target in the area, you can take a Reaction to grant advantage to the attack roll. If the attack hits, it deals an extra 1d8 Force damage.

entries.name: Unicorn Spirit.
entries: When you cast a spell using a spell slot that restores hit points, each creature of your choice in the area regains 1d8 hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Mighty Summoner
entries: You always have the Summon Beast and Summon Fey spells prepared. Whenever you cast these spells, the summoned creature gains the following benefits.

entries.name: Totem Resilience.
entries: The creature gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Druid level.

entries.name: Spirits Vengeance.
entries: Whenever the creature hits with an attack roll, it deals extra damage equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Guardian Spirit
entries: Your Spirit Totem safeguards the beasts and fey that you call forth with your magic. When a Beast or Fey that you summoned with a spell ends its turn in your Spirit Totem aura, it regains a number of hit points equal to your Druid level.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Faithful Summons
entries: The spirits of nature protect you in the moment of danger. If you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can immediately cast Summon Fey as if cast with a level 9 spell slot. This summoning requires no Concentration and the spirit lasts for its full duration or until dismissed (no action required). The spirit acts to protect you and attack your foes. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Circle of Spores Spells
entries: Your symbiotic link to fungi and your ability to tap into the cycle of life and death grants you access to certain spells. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the spells listed for that level in the Circle of Spores Spells table. Once you gain access to one of these spells, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Halo of Spores
entries: You are surrounded by a 10–foot Emanation of necrotic spores that are harmless until you unleash them. When a creature you can see moves into the Emanation or starts its turn there, you can take a Reaction to deal 1d6 Acid, Necrotic, Poison damage to that creature. The damage increases to 1d8 at level 6, 1d10 at level 10, and 1d12 at level 14.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Symbiotic Entity
entries: You gain the ability to magically empower your spores. As an Action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to gain the following benefits for 10 minutes or until or have the Incapacitated condition.

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to two times your Druid level.
The radius of the Emanation of your Halo of Spores is increased to 15 feet.

Whenever the Emanation of Halo of Spores enters the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Constitution saving throw. The creature takes your Halo of Spores damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Life Siphon
entries: Your spores rapidly break down organic matter to nourish your allies. When a creature takes damage from your Halo of Spores, you can choose one creature within your Emanation. That creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the damage dealt plus your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Displacing Spores
entries: Your spores can hide your allies and protect them from attacks. While your Symbiotic Entity is active, every creature of your choice within the Emanation has half cover.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Fungal Body
entries: The fungal spores in your body alter you: you can't be Blinded, Frightened, or Poisoned, and have resistance to Necrotic and Poison damage. In addition, any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit, unless you're incapacitated.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: You gain proficiency with brewer's supplies and one of the following skills of your choice: Acrobatics, Performance, Persuasion.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Drunken Technique
entries: Your fighting style mimics the unpredictable swaying of a drunkard. Whenever you use Flurry of Blows, your Speed increases by 10 feet and your movement doesn’t provoke Opportunity Attacks until the end of your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Mystic Brew
entries: You have learned to imbue your drinks with magic. When you finish a Short or Long Rest while holding Brewer’s Supplies, you can magically produce one of the following beverages: Cinnamon Dragon, Heavenly Spirit or Drunkard’s Luck. As a Magic Action you can drink the beverage and gain its effect for 1 hour.

entries.name: Cinnamon Dragon.
entries: You can take a Magic action to exhale flame. Each creature in a 30–foot Cone must make a Dexterity saving throw taking Fire damage equal to three rolls of your Martial Arts die on a failed save or half as much on a successful one.

entries.name: Heavenly Spirit.
entries: You gain Resistance to Necrotic and Radiant damage.

entries.name: Refreshing Dip.
entries: Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain additional Hit Points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Master Brewer
entries: The following beverages are added to your Mystic Brew options.

entries.name: Blue Lightning.
entries: Whenever you take a Reaction that isn’t making an Opportunity Attack or casting a spell, you can make one Unarmed Strike as part of that Reaction.

entries.name: Drunkard’s Luck.
entries: Once every turn, when you fail a d20 Test, you can take a Reaction to reroll the test with Advantage and must use the new roll.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Intoxicated Frenzy
entries: You gain the ability to make an overwhelming number of attacks against a group of enemies. When you use your Flurry of Blows, you can make up to three additional attacks with it (up to a total of six Flurry of Blows attacks), provided that each Flurry of Blows attack targets a different creature this turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Monster Slayer Magic
entries: You learn an additional spell when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown in the Monster Slayer Spells table. The spell counts as a ranger spell for you, but it doesn't count against the number of ranger spells you know.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Slayer’s Sense
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can focus your senses to detect the unnatural. For the next 10 minutes or until you have the Incapacitated condition, you know the location of any Aberration, Fey, Fiend, or Undead within 60 feet of yourself, and you know its creature type. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Slayer’s Prey
entries: Once every turn, when you hit the target of your Hunter's Mark with a weapon attack, you can choose one of the following options.

items: You deal an extra 1d6 Force damage.
You reduce the target Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Supernatural Defence
entries: You gain extra resilience against your prey's assaults on your mind and body. Whenever the target of your Hunter’s Mark forces you to make a saving throw, you can add 1d6 to the roll.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Esoteric Recall
entries: As a Magic Action, you cast the Legend Lore spell without expending a spell slot or needing Material components. You can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Magic–User Nemesis
entries: You have learned to disrupt the flow of magic and escape attempts just before they materialize. When you see a creature casting a spell or teleporting within 60 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to make one weapon attack against it. If the attack hits, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell or teleport is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended. In addition, if this attack reduces the target to 0 hit points, the target cannot return to its original plane. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest, unless you expend a spell slot of level 3+ (no action required) to restore the use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Slayer’s Counter
entries: Your readiness against enemy magic and abilities is absolute. If the target of your Hunter's Mark forces you to make a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to make one weapon attack against it. If the attack hits, your save automatically succeeds.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Nature Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Nature Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Nature Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Absorb Elements|xge}, {@spell Beast Sense|xphb}, {@spell Speak with Animals|xphb}, {@spell Spike Growth|xphb}, {@spell Thorn Whip|xphb}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Acolyte of Nature
entries: You know {@language Primordial} and gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Nature Teachings.} You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Survival}.
{@b Primal Warrior.} When you cast the {@spell Thorn Whip|xphb} cantrip, you can change its damage type to one of the following damage types: Cold, Fire, Lightning or Thunder.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Elemental Surge
entries: As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity to manifest elemental energy around you in a 15-foot Emanation. When you use this feature, choose one of the following benefits that lasts for 1 minute or until you are {@condition incapacitated}.

items: {@b Air.} Whenever a creature of your choice starts its turn within the Emanation, its Speed increases by 10 feet and has {@variantrule Half Cover|xphb} until the start of its next turn.

{@b Earth.} When you activate this feature, each creature of your choice within the Emanation gains Temporary Hit Points equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Wisdom modifier. In addition, the ground in the Emanation is Difficult Terrain for your enemies.

{@b Fire.} Whenever a creature enters the Emanation for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, you can force it to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 2d6} Fire damage.

{@b Water.} Whenever a creature enters the Emanation for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 1d6} Bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Nature Guardian
entries: You learnt to fight like a raging elemental. You can cast the {@spell Thorn Whip|xphb} cantrip as a Bonus Action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Elemental Sovereign
entries: You become an avatar of primordial forces. You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Planar Attunement.} When you finish a Long Rest you gain immunity to one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Lightning, Fire or Thunder.
{@b Dual Surge.} When you use your Elemental Surge Channel Divinity, you can manifest two elements simultaneously, granting you both benefits for the duration.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tempest Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Tempest Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Tempest Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Fog Cloud|xphb}, {@spell Gust of Wind|xphb}, {@spell Shatter|xphb}, {@spell Thunderous Smite|xphb}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Wrath of the Storm
entries: You can thunderously rebuke attackers. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can take a Reaction to force the attacker to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 2d8} Cold, Lightning or Thunder damage (your choice), and half as much damage on a successful one. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Destructive Wrath
entries: You wield the power of the storm with unchecked ferocity. When you deal Cold, Lightning or Thunder damage to a creature, you can spend a use of your Channel Divinity to deal extra damage equal to your Cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Thunderbolt Strike
entries: When you deal Cold, Lightning or Thunder damage to a Large or smaller creature, you can also push it up to 10 feet from you horizontally.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Stormborn
entries: You’re blessed with the power of the storm and gain a Fly speed equal to your walking speed. In addition, When you finish a Long Rest you gain immunity to one of the following damage types: Cold, Lightning, or Thunder.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Insightful Fighting
entries: You can decipher a creature's tactics to counter them. When you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you gain the following benefits for 10 minutes.

items: When you deal Sneak Attack damage against the target, you deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage.
You know whether the target has any resistances or immunities and what they are.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Steady Senses
entries: You developed a talent to unravel lies and secrets. You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Adept Detective.} You gain proficiency in {@skill Insight} or {@skill Investigation} checks.

{@b Supernatural Perception.} You can cast either {@spell Detect Magic|xphb} or {@spell Identify|xphb} without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Insightful Strikes
entries: The following effects are added to your Cunning Strike options.

items: {@b Bleed Out (Cost: 3d6).} For 1 minute, the target takes {@damage 3d4} necrotic damage at the start of its turn. At the end of each of its turns, it must make a Constitution saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

{@b Weaken (Costs: 2d6).} The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it has disadvantage on Strength–based D20 Tests and subtracts {@dice 1d6} from all its damage rolls until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 13: Unerring Eye
entries: As an action, you gain Truesight with a range of 30 feet for 10 minutes. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Short or Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Eye for Weakness
entries: You know how to exploit a creature's weaknesses by carefully studying its tactics. The extra damage dealt by Insightful Fighting increases to {@damage 3d6}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Master of Intrigue
entries: You gain proficiency with the {@item disguise kit|xphb} and the {@item forgery kit|xphb}. Moreover, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill and can unerringly mimic the voice and accent of a creature that you hear speak for 1 minute. A creature is able to know you’re faking with a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check contested by your Charisma ({@skill Deception}).

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Master of Tactics
entries: Once every turn, you can use one of the following options:

items: {@b Pawn’s Opening.} Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage, you can take the {@action Help} action on a creature within 30 feet of you (no action required).

{@b Knight’s Defense.} Immediately after you deal your Sneak Attack damage, a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you can use its reaction to move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Superior Tactics
entries: The following effects are added to your Cunning Strike options.

items: {@b Rook’s Castling (Cost: 1d6).} You push the target of the attack up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line, provided that it is up to one size larger than you.
{@b Bishop’s Check (Cost: 2d6).} The target has disadvantage on the first saving throw it makes before the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 13: Misdirection
entries: You’ve become the perfect schemer. You gain the following benefits:

items: When a creature reads your mind, you can present false thoughts by succeeding on a Charisma ({@skill Deception}) check contested by a Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check.
When you use Uncanny Dodge, you can choose a creature within 5 feet of you and force it to take the same amount of damage you take.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Final Tactic
entries: The following effect is added to your Cunning Strike options.

items: {@b King’s Gambit (6d6).} Both you and the target of your Sneak Attack become vulnerable to the next instance of damage you take before the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Skirmisher
entries: You are difficult to pin down. When an enemy ends its turn within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to move up to your Speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Survivalist
entries: You gain proficiency and expertise in the {@skill Nature} and {@skill Survival} skills.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Superior Mobility
entries: You move fast while surrounded and slash through your foes with every step. Your speed increases by 10 feet. Also, any creature of your choice takes {@damage 1d6} slashing damage when you enter a space within 5 feet of it. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 13: Ambush Master
entries: The following effects are added to your Cunning Strike options.

items: {@b Smoke Cloud (Cost: 1d6).} You create a 10–foot radius sphere centered on the target of your attack. The smoke lasts until the end of your next turn.
{@b Ambush (Costs: 2d6).} Until the start of your next turn, each attack made against the target of your Sneak Attack gains advantage.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Sudden Strike
entries: You can strike with deadly speed. If you take the {@action Attack} Action on your turn, you can make one additional attack as a Bonus Action. This attack can benefit from your Sneak Attack even if you have already used it this turn, but you can't use your Sneak Attack against the same target more than once in a turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Fancy Footwork
entries: You learn how to land a strike and then slip away without reprisal. During your turn, if you make a melee attack against a creature, it can't make opportunity attacks against you until the end of your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Rakish Audacity
entries: Your confidence propels you into battle. When you roll initiative, you can add your Charisma modifier to the roll. Also, you gain an additional way to use your Sneak Attack. You don't need advantage to use Sneak Attack against a creature if you are within 5 feet of it, no other creatures are within 5 feet of you, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Panache
entries: Your charm becomes extremely beguiling. As a Bonus Action, you can force a creature within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save the creature suffers one of the following effects.

items: {@b Awe.} A friendly creature is {@condition charmed} by you for 10 minutes.
{@b Provoke.} An hostile creature has disadvantage on all attack rolls against creatures other than you.

subclassFeature.name: Level 13: Dashing Strikes
entries: The following effects are added to your Cunning Strike options.

items: {@b Parrying Stance (Cost: 2d6).} You roll {@dice 1d6} and add its result as a bonus to your armor class until the start of your next turn.
{@b Invigorate (Costs: 2d6).} A creature within 30 feet of you, can add {@dice 1d6} to its D20 tests until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Master Duelist
entries: Immediately after, you deal your Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can make an attack with a melee weapon against that creature as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Horizon Walker Spells
entries: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the following table, you thereafter always have the listed spells always prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Planar Perception
entries: You gain the ability to magically sense the presence of planar anomalies. As a Magic Action you gain one of the following features.

items: {@b Traveler.} You know the location of each aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, or fiend within 60 feet of you.
{@b Portal.} You detect the distance and direction to the closest portal within 1 mile of you.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Planar Warrior
entries: You draw on the energy of the multiverse to empower your attacks. Once every turn, when you hit with a weapon, you can change all the attack's damage to force and deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage to the target. This extra damage increases to {@damage 1d8} when you reach Ranger level 11.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Distant Strikes
entries: You gain the ability to pass between the planes in a blink of an eye. When you take the Attack Action, you can teleport up to 10 feet before or after each attack to an unoccupied space you can see. Also, if you attack two different creatures during the Attack Action, you can make one additional attack as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Ethereal Step
entries: As a Bonus Action, you briefly step through the planes. Until the start of your next turn, you have a Fly speed equal to your Speed and have resistance to all damage except Force damage. You can also move through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain, but you take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses of this feature when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Create Portal
entries: You can create portals to quickly teleport you and your allies. As a Magic Action you can teleport each willing creature of your choice within 10 feet of you to an unoccupied space within 500 feet of you. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long rest unless you expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required) to restore the use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Drakewarden Spells
entries: When you reach a Ranger level specified in the following table, you thereafter always have the listed spells always prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Draconic Gift
entries: Your bond with dragons empowers your presence. You gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Dragon Presence.} You learn the {@spell Thaumaturgy|xphb} cantrip, it is a Ranger spell for you.
{@b Dragon Tongue.} You know {@language Draconic} or one other language of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Drake Companion
entries: You magically summon a drake, which draws strength from your bond with dragons. You choose the drake appearance, choosing one appropriate to its stat block. The drake is friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die.

{@b The Drake in Combat.} In combat, the drake acts during your turn. It can move and take Reactions, but the only action it takes is the Dodge action unless you take a Bonus Action to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition, the drake acts on its own and isn't limited to the Dodge action.
{@b Restoring the Drake.} If the drake has died, you can take a Magic action to touch it and expend a spell slot. The drake returns to life after 1 minute with all its Hit Points restored.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Bond of Fang and Scale
entries: The bond you share with your drake intensifies, you gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Drake Mount.} The drake grows to Medium size. You can mount it if your size is Medium or smaller. While you are riding your drake, it can't use its Fly speed.
{@b Fang Burst.} The drake attacks deal {@damage 1d6} extra damage of its Dragon Essence's type.
{@b Shared Resistance.} You are resistant to the damage of the drake's Dragon Essence.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Drake's Breath
entries: When you take the attack action, you can forgo one attack to exhale a 30-foot cone energy breath or cause your drake to exhale it. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning or Poison damage. Each creature in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 8d6} damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of level 3+ to restore the use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Perfected Bond
entries: While your drake is summoned, you and the drake gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Empowered Bite.} The drake's Bite attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage instead of {@damage 1d6}.
{@b Large Drake.} The drake grows to Large size and can use its fly while mounted.

{@b Reflexive Resistance.} When you or the drake take damage while you're within 30 feet of each other, you can take a Reaction to give yourself or the drake resistance to that damage. You can do so a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Storm Magic
entries: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the following table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

rows: {@spell Feather Fall|xphb}, {@spell Fog Cloud|xphb}, {@spell Gust of Wind|xphb}, {@spell Rime's Binding Ice|ftd}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Wind Speaker
entries: You know Primordial or another language of your choice. Also, as a Bonus Action you cause one of the following effects until the end of your next turn.

items: You cause rain to start or stop falling within a 100-foot emanation.
You make wind blow in a chosen direction within a 100-foot emanation.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tempestuous Magic
entries: Immediately before or after you cast a Sorcerer spell of level 1+, you can fly up to 10 feet without provoking opportunity attacks and force a creature within 30 feet of you to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage. At Sorcerer level 6 this damage becomes {@damage 2d6}, {@damage 3d6} at level 14, and {@damage 4d6} at level 18.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Heart of the Storm
entries: You gain resistance to Lightning and Thunder damage. In addition, when you use your Tempestuous Magic, you can target up to two creatures within 30 feet of you instead of one.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Wind Soul
entries: You gain 60 feet of Fly speed. As a Magic Action, you can reduce your Fly speed to 30 feet and choose a number of creatures within 60 feet of you equal to your Charisma modifier. The chosen creatures gain 30 feet of Fly speed for 8 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Level 18: Storm's Fury
entries: You embody the full might of a storm, you gain the following benefits.

items: You have immunity to Lightning and Thunder damage.

If you drop to 0 Hit Points and don't die outright, you regain a number of hit points equal to your Sorcerer level. In addition, each creature of your choice within 30-foot emanation must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} Thunder damage and is pushed 15 feet from you. On a success, it only takes half that damage. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long rest, unless you spend 5 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore the use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Lesser Natural Control
entries: You learn the {@spell elementalism|xphb} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting modifier for it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Storm Aura
entries: Whenever you activate your Rage, choose Desert, Sea, or Tundra. You extend an aura in a 10-foot Emanation originating from yourself for the duration of your Rage. Your aura has an effect based on your choice:

items.name: Desert
entries: Roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage damage bonus and add them together. Each creature of your choice in the aura must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take fire damage equal to the total.

items.name: Sea
entries: Roll a number of d8s equal to your Rage damage bonus and add them together. One creature of your choice within your aura must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking lightning damage equal to the total on a failed save.

items.name: Tundra
entries: Roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage damage bonus and add them together. One creature of your choice within your aura must succeed on a Strength saving throw or it subtracts the number rolled from the next attack roll or ability check it makes before the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Storm Soul
entries: When you finish a Long or Short rest, choose one of the following benefits that lasts until you use this feature again.

items.name: Desert
entries: You gain Resistance to Fire damage, and as a Magic action you can touch a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried and cause it to start burning.

items.name: Sea
entries: You gain Resistance to Lightning damage, can breathe underwater, and gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

items.name: Tundra
entries: You gain Resistance to Cold damage. As a Magic action you can touch water to turn an unoccupied 5-foot Cube of it into ice, which melts after 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Raging Storm
entries: The power of the storm you channel grows mightier, lashing out at your foes. The effect is based on the environment you choose for your Storm Aura.

items.name: Desert
entries: When a creature fails its saving throw against your Storm Aura effect, it is engulfed with flames. For 1 minute the target takes {@damage 2d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns, or until it uses an action to make a Dexterity check against your Rage DC to extinguish the flames.

items.name: Sea
entries: When you deal your Storm Aura effect damage, you can choose another creature within 30 feet of the original target. The new target makes the same Dexterity saving throw as the original.

items.name: Tundra
entries: When a creature fails the saving throw against your Storm Aura effect, it takes {@damage 2d6} cold damage and its speed becomes 0 until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Shielding Aura
entries: You gain immunity instead of resistance to the damage type of your Storm Soul feature. Moreover, each creature of your choice within a 15-foot Emanation from you gains the benefits of your Storm Soul resistance.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Giant's Power
entries: You know {@language Giant} or another language of your choice, if you already know it. You learn the {@spell druidcraft|xphb} or {@spell thaumaturgy|xphb} cantrip and Strength is your spellcasting modifier for it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Giant's Havoc
entries: Your rage channels the primal might of giants. While raging, you gain the following benefits:

items.name: Improved Reach
entries: The reach of your attacks is increased by 5 feet.

items.name: Mighty Stature
entries: If you are smaller than Large, you become Large along with anything you are wearing. If you lack the room to become Large, your size doesn't change.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Elemental Cleaver
entries: You bond with the elemental might of giants to infuse weapons with primordial energy. When you enter your rage, you can give the following benefits to a weapon you're holding.

items.name: Primal Power
entries: Choose one of the following damage types: Cold, Fire, Lightning or Thunder. Once every turn, the weapon deals {@damage 1d12} extra damage of the chosen type.

items.name: Returning
entries: The weapon gains the thrown property (20/60). If you throw the weapon, it reappears in your hand the instant after it hits or misses a target.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Mighty Impel
entries: Your strength allows you to hurl creatures on the battlefield. While raging, you can use a Bonus Action to force a Large or smaller creature within your reach to make a Strength saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you move the creature 5 feet in a direction of your choice, and you can move up to 5 feet in that direction immediately after.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Demiurgic Colossus
entries: The primordial power of your rage intensifies. When you rage, your reach increases by 10 feet, your size can increase to Large or Huge (your choice) and the extra damage dealt by your Elemental Cleaver becomes {@damage 2d12}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Psychic Blades
entries: Your mastery of psychic magic gives you the following benefits.
You learn the {@spell shadow blade|xphb} spell and it counts as a Bard spell for you.
When you hit with a weapon attack or deal Psychic damage you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to deal extra Psychic damage equal to three rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Words of Terror
entries: If you speak for 1 minute to a creature with an Intelligence ability score of at least 6, you can attempt to seed paranoia in its mind. At the end of the conversation, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell DC or it takes {@damage 1d6} psychic damage and becomes {@condition frightened} of you. The {@condition frightened} effect ends if the target ends a turn without seeing you or experiencing harm from you.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Mantle of Whispers
entries: When a Medium or Small creature within 30 feet of you reaches 0 hit points, you steal its shadow. You can take a Bonus Action to spend the shadow and gain the following benefits.

items.name: Dark Resilience
entries: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Bard level.

items.name: Psychic Whispers
entries: For 1 hour, you take the creature's appearance and learn general details on the creature's background and personal life.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Mischievous Afterimage
entries: Immediately after you deal damage with your Psychic Blades feature, you can teleport 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Shadow Lore
entries: As a Magic Action, you whisper in the mind of a creature within 30 feet of you. It must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell DC. On a failed save, your whispers sound like the target's own doubts and fears magnified. The target takes {@damage 8d6} psychic damage and becomes {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Arcane Lore
entries: You studied magical theory and secrets of nature, granting you the following benefits. You know either the {@spell druidcraft|xphb} or {@spell prestidigitation|xphb} cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Arcane Shot
entries: You learn to unleash special magical effects with your shots.

{@b Arcane Shot Options.} You learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice from the "Arcane Shot Options" of this subclass. You learn an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th levels in this class.

{@b Using Arcane Shot.} Once per turn when you make a ranged attack using a weapon with the ammunition property, you can apply one Arcane Shot option to that attack. You decide to use the option when you hit or miss.
{@b Arcane Shot Die.} The dice used by your Arcane Shot Options is a d6. The Arcane Shot Die becomes a d8 at level 10, a d10 at level 15 and a d12 at level 18.
{@b Saving Throws.} If an Arcane Shot option requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Intelligence modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Arcane Shot Options
entries: The Arcane Shot options are presented here in alphabetical order.

items.name: Banishing Shot
entries: The target of the attack takes Psychic damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot die and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished to the Ethereal Plane until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Bursting Shot
entries: The target of the attack and each creature within a 10-foot emanation originating from it takes Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane shot die.

items.name: Enfeebling Shot
entries: The target of the attack takes Necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane shot die and must succeed on a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, each damage roll of the target is reduced by the amount of Necrotic damage dealt until the end of its next turn.

items.name: Grasping Shot
entries: The target of the attack takes Slashing damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the {@condition restrained} condition for 1 minute.

items.name: Piercing Shot
entries: You don't make an attack roll for the attack. Each creature in a 30-foot long and 5-foot wide line originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Curving Shot
entries: You learn how to direct an errant arrow toward a new target. If you make an attack roll with a weapon with the ammunition property and miss, you can cause the shot to ricochet as a Bonus Action. Select a new target within 60 feet of the original target and make a new attack roll. On a hit, the new target takes damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Magical Ammunition
entries: When you shoot an ammunition, you can imbue it with one of the following properties.

items.name: Darkening Shot
entries: Magical darkness fills a 15-foot emanation originating from the ammunition for 1 minute.

items.name: Unlocking Shot
entries: A burst of magic fills a 15-foot emanation originating from the ammunition. Any mundane lock in the emanation becomes unlocked.

items.name: Vine Shot
entries: A 60-foot long vine grows from the ammunition. You and other creatures can then climb it. The vine withers away after 10 minutes.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short or Long rest. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a use of your Second Wind.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Ever-Ready Shot
entries: When you roll initiative you can regain one expended use of Arcane Shot.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Arcane Burst
entries: When you use your Indomitable feature, each creature of your choice within a 10-foot emanation originating from you must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Arcane Shot DC or be pushed 15 feet away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Level 18: Arcane Evasion
entries: When a creature misses you with an attack, you can take a reaction to teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can then make a ranged attack roll against the attacker as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Elegant Courtier
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill History}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion}. In addition, whenever you make a Charisma check using a skill you are proficient in, you gain a bonus equal to your Wisdom modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Fighting Spirit
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} Action, you can give yourself advantage on every attack you make in this turn and gain a number of temporary hit points equal to your Fighter level. You can use this feature once, regaining expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 7: Focussed Fighter
entries: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you instead gain proficiency in Intelligence or Charisma saving throws (your choice).

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Rapid Strike
entries: Once every turn, as part of the {@action Attack} Action, when you make an attack with advantage, you can forgo the advantage to make an additional weapon attack as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Tireless Spirit
entries: When you roll initiative you regain expended uses of Fighting Spirit until you have 2, if you have 1 or fewer.

subclassFeature.name: Level 18: Strength Before Death
entries: When you drop to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to delay falling {@condition unconscious} and immediately take an extra turn. Taking damage causes death saves failure and can kill you. At the end of this extra turn, you fall {@condition unconscious}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Kensei Weapon
entries: Choose one melee and one ranged weapon to be your kensei weapons. They can be any weapon that lacks the heavy and special properties, or a Longbow. You are proficient in them, can use their properties with your martial arts features, and they count as monk weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Path of the Kensei
entries: You gain proficiency in calligrapher's supplies or painter's supplies. In addition, once every turn, you can gain one of the following benefits:

items.name: Agile Parry
entries: While you're holding a melee kensei weapon and are not {@condition incapacitated}, you gain a +2 bonus to AC.

items.name: Kensei's Shot
entries: When you hit with a ranged kensei weapon as part of the {@action Attack} Action, you can deal extra damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: One with the Blade
entries: You extend your power into your kensei weapons, granting you the following benefits.

items.name: Kensei Blow
entries: You can make an attack with a kensei weapon as a Bonus Action.

items.name: Deft Strike
entries: Once every turn, when you hit a target with a kensei weapon, you can spend 1 Focus Point to deal extra damage equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Sharpen the Blade
entries: At the start of your turn, you can expend up to 3 Focus Points to empower one kensei weapon you touch. That weapon gains a bonus to attack and damage rolls equal the number of discipline points spent until the end of your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Kensei Master
entries: At the peak of your fighting prowess, you gain the following benefits.

items.name: Kensei Flurry
entries: You can expend 1 Focus Point to make two attacks with a Kensei Weapon as a Bonus Action.

items.name: Unerring Accuracy
entries: Your mastery of weapons grants you extraordinary accuracy. If you miss with an attack roll using a monk weapon on your turn, you can reroll it. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Radiant Awakening
entries: At the start of your turn, you can spend 1 discipline point to wreathe yourself in radiant energy for 10 minutes. For the duration you gain the following benefits.

items: Once every turn, when you hit with an attack, you can deal extra radiant damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die.

Once every turn, you can replace an Unarmed Strike attack to make a ranged attack using Dexterity against a creature within 60 feet of you. If the attack hits, it deals radiant damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Searing Arc Strike
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend 1 Focus Point to emit a 15-foot cone of light from your hand. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save it takes radiant damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die, or half as much on a successful one.

subclassFeature.name: Level 11: Searing Sunburst
entries: As a Magic Action, you hurl a bolt of light at a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Each creature in that 20-foot radius sphere centered on that point must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} radiant damage, or half as much on a successful one.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Sun Avatar
entries: You become an avatar of radiant energy. You gain immunity to radiant damage and once per turn when you hit with an attack that deals radiant damage, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Benign Transposition
entries: As a Bonus Action, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a willing creature within 30 feet, you both teleport swapping places. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Conjuration Savant
entries: Choose two Wizard spells from the Conjuration school, each of which must be no higher than level 1 or 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Conjuration school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Distant Transposition
entries: The range of your Benign Transposition feature increases to 60 feet. Additionally, you now regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Short or Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Durable Summons
entries: When you cast a Conjuration spell to summon or create a creature using a spell slot, that creature gains temporary hit points equal to twice your Wizard level when it first appears. While it has these temporary hit points, the creature has resistance to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic and Radiant.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Focussed Conjuration
entries: Taking damage can’t break your concentration on Conjuration spells.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Splintered Summons
entries: When you cast {@spell Summon Construct|xphb}, {@spell Summon Dragon|xphb}, {@spell Summon Elemental|xphb} or {@spell Summon Fey|xphb} using a spell slot, you can modify the spell to summon two creatures instead of one. Each creature uses the stat block and rules denoted by the spell and appears in a different unoccupied space of your choice within the spell’s range, but the summoned creatures’ hit points are halved. If you lose concentration on the spell, both creatures disappear.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Enchanting Voice
entries: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1). You also gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation} or {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Enchantment Savant
entries: Choose two Wizard spells from the Enchantment school, each of which must be no higher than level 1 or 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Enchantment school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Hypnotic Presence
entries: As a Magic Action, choose one creature that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. If the target can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition charmed} condition for 1 minute or until it takes damage. While {@condition charmed} in this way, the target has the {@condition incapacitated} condition and a speed of 0. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Split Enchantment
entries: When you use your Hypnotic Presence feature or cast an Enchantment spell that targets only one creature, you can target one additional creature with it. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Instinctive Charm
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see hits deals damage to you, you can take a Reaction to force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the damage you take is reduced to 0 and another creature of your choice within 30 feet takes the same amount of damage. Once you use this Reaction, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long rest. You can restore your use of this feature by casting an Enchantment spell with a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Superior Enchantment
entries: When you use your Hypnotic Presence feature or cast an Enchantment spell that causes the {@condition charmed} condition, you can ignore immunities to the {@condition charmed} condition. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Necromancy Savant
entries: Choose two Wizard spells from the Necromancy school, each of which must be no higher than level 1 or 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Necromancy school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Necromancy Spellbook
entries: Your mastery of necromantic lore grants you extra abilities.

items.name: Necrotic Resistance
entries: You gain resistance to Necrotic damage.

items.name: Grim Harvest
entries: When you cast a Necromancy spell using a spell slot, you can choose an undead creature you can see within 60 feet of you and make it regain hit points equal to the level of the spell slot expended plus your Wizard level.

items.name: Undead Familiar
entries: {@spell Find Familiar|xphb} is added to your spellbook. When you cast the spell, you can choose the Skeleton or Zombie forms in addition to the normal forms.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Undead Thralls
entries: {@spell Summon Undead|xphb} is added to your spellbook and you always have it prepared. Whenever you cast the spell, the summoned creature gains the following benefits.

items.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: The creature’s hit point maximum and current hit points increase by a number equal to the level of the spell slot used plus your Intelligence modifier.

items.name: Withering Strike
entries: Whenever the creature hits with an attack roll, it deals extra damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one).

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Harvest Undead
entries: You have learned more secrets about the nuances of life and death. Immediately after you become bloodied but not outright killed, you can take a Reaction to reduce an Undead creature under your control to 0 hit points. You then immediately regain a number of hit points equal to your Wizard level.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Death's Master
entries: Abstruse rituals within your spellbook allow you mastery over forces of death. While holding your spellbook you gain the following benefits.

items.name: Bolster Undead
entries: As a Bonus Action, an undead creature you have created or summoned within 60 feet of you gains temporary hit points equal to your Wizard level. A creature can gain these temporary hit points only once every 24 hours.

items.name: Burst Undead
entries: When an undead creature under your control is reduced to 0 hit points, you can cause it to explode with necrotic energy. Roll a number of d6s equal to your Intelligence modifier and add them together. Each creature in a 10-foot emanation originating from the undead makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC. A target takes Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled on a failed save and half as much on a successful one.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Transmutation Savant
entries: Choose two Wizard spells from the Transmutation school, each of which must be no higher than level 1 or 2, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Transmutation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Transmuter's Stone
entries: When you finish a Long rest, you can create a magic stone that lasts until you use this feature again. A creature carrying the stone gains proficiency in Constitution saving throws and one of the following benefits chosen when you create the stone.

items.name: Darkvision
entries: The bearer gains darkvision with a range of 60 feet or increases the range of its darkvision by 60 feet.

items.name: Resistance
entries: The bearer gains resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison or Thunder damage (you choose each time you choose this benefit).

items.name: Speed
entries: The bearer’s speed increases by 10 feet.
You can change the stone’s benefit when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Empowered Transmutation
entries: When you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot, you can treat the spell as if it were cast using a spell slot 1 level above the slot expended. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Potent Stone
entries: Your Transmuter’s Stone is more versatile. When you create it, you can choose up to two benefits. You can choose each option other than Resistance only once, and if you choose Resistance twice, you must choose different damage types. You can change either or both benefits when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot. In addition, the following are now among your benefit options for Transmuter’s Stone.

items.name: Evasive
entries: The bearer can take the disengage action as a Bonus Action.

items.name: Tremorsense
entries: The bearer gains Tremorsense with a range of 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Level 10: Shapechanger
entries: You always have the {@spell Polymorph|xphb} spell prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a Long Rest. In addition, when you target yourself with the spell, you can modify the spell to gain the benefits below.

items.name: Game Statistics
entries: You retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak. You also retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats.

items.name: Transmute Spells
entries: You can cast Transmutation spells while shapeshifted, except for any spell with a Material component that has a specified cost.

subclassFeature.name: Level 14: Master Transmuter
entries: While you carry your Transmuter’s Stone, you can take a Magic Action to consume the reserve of transmutation magic stored inside and choose one of the following benefits.
