After you use your Transmuter’s Stone in this way, the stone crumbles to dust. You can prevent the stone’s destruction by expending a level 5+ spell slot.

items.name: Major Transmutation
entries: An object you touch— no larger than a 10-foot Cube or eight connected 5-foot Cubes —and transmute it into another nonmagical object of similar size and mass and of equal or lesser value.

items.name: Panacea
entries: A creature you touch regains a number of Hit Points equal to half its Hit Point maximum (round down). Any curse affecting the target is lifted. If the target has the {@condition poisoned} or {@condition petrified} conditions, those conditions end.

items.name: Restore Life
entries: You cast the {@spell Raise Dead|xphb} spell without expending a spell slot, using the stone in place of the required Material components.

items.name: Restore Youth
entries: A willing creature you touch permanently appears {@dice 3d10} years younger, to a minimum of young adulthood.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Twilight Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at the cleric levels listed in the Twilight Domain Spells table. See the Divine Domain class feature for how domain spells work.

entries.caption: Twilight Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Faerie Fire|xphb}, {@spell Moonbeam|xphb}, {@spell Sleep|xphb}, {@spell See Invisibility|xphb}
{@spell Aura of Vitality|xphb}, {@spell Tiny Hut|xphb|Leomund's Tiny Hut}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Eyes of Night
entries: As a Magic action, you touch a creature and bless it with eyes that see through the deepest gloom. For 1 hour, it gains Darkvision with a range of 120 feet and can see through magical darkness. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 2+ level to restore the use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Twilight Sanctuary
entries: As a Magic Action, you present your holy symbol and emanate a sphere of twilight. The sphere is a 30-foot radius emanation filled with dim light. It moves with you, and lasts for 1 minute or until you are {@condition incapacitated}. Whenever a creature ends its turn within the area, you can grant that creature temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Proficiency Bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Level 6: Improved Sanctuary
entries: While your Twilight Sanctuary is active, you gain the following additional benefits.

items: You gain Fly speed equal to your Speed.
Whenever a creature ends its turn within the area, you can choose to end one effect on it causing it to be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, instead of granting temporary hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Level 17: Twilight Shroud
entries: The twilight that you summon offers a protective embrace. You and your allies have half cover while in the emanation created by your Twilight Sanctuary.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item alchemist's supplies|xphb} or another type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Alchemist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Alchemist Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

rows: {@spell Melf's Acid Arrow|xphb}, {@spell Prayer of Healing|xphb}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Experimental Elixir
entries: You can prepare special vials to contain powerful potions called Experimental Elixirs. You can only have a number of vials up to your Intelligence modifier. When you finish a Long rest, you can fill your vials with elixirs rolling on the Experimental Elixir Table. At the start of your turn spend a spell slot and fill a number of vials equal to the spell slot level spent with Experimental Elixirs of your choice. A creature holding a vial can drink the elixir or administer it to a willing creature as a Bonus Action.

rows: {@b Healing.} The drinker regains a number of hit points equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Intelligence modifier. The number of Hit Points restored increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach Artificer levels 9 ({@dice 3d8}) and 15 ({@dice 4d8}).
{@b Swiftness.} The drinker's Speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour. This extra speed increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (15 feet) and 15 (20 feet).
{@b Resilience.} The drinker gains a +1 bonus to AC for 10 minutes. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (1 hour) and 15 (8 hours).

{@b Boldness.} The drinker can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to every attack roll and saving throw they make for the next minute. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (10 minutes) and 15 (1 hour).
{@b Flight.} The drinker gains 15 feet of Fly Speed for 10 minutes. The Fly Speed increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (20 feet) and 15 (30 feet).
{@b Transformation.} The drinker gains the effect of {@spell Alter Self|xphb} for 10 minutes. The duration increases when you reach Artificer levels 9 (1 hour) and 15 (8 hours).

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Alchemical Savant
entries: You're an expert in the use of chemicals to heal or harm. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Empowered Alchemy
entries: You can add your Intelligence modifier to one roll of your Artificer spells that restore hit points or deal acid, fire or poison damage.

items.name: Chemical Toss
entries: As a Bonus Action, make a spell attack against a creature within 30 feet of you. On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 2d6} + your Intelligence modifier acid damage.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Restorative Reagents
entries: Your potions gain restorative properties. When a creature gains the effect of one of your Experimental Elixirs, it gains temporary hit points equal to {@dice 2d6} + your Intelligence modifier. Also, you can end one effect causing it to be {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Paralyzed}, or {@condition Poisoned}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Chemical Mastery
entries: Your neverending research on alchemy grants you the following benefits.

items.name: Chemical Resistance
entries: You gain Resistance to Acid damage and Poison damage. You also gain Immunity to the {@condition Poisoned} condition.

items.name: Conjured Cauldron
entries: You can cast {@spell Tasha's Bubbling Cauldron|xphb} without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without Material components, provided you use Alchemist's Supplies as the Spellcasting Focus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tools of the Trade
entries: You gain proficiency with heavy armor. You also gain proficiency with {@item smith's tools|xphb} or another type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Armorer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Armorer Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Arcane Armor
entries: When you don a suit of armor you can transform it into your Arcane Armor. You gain the following benefits while wearing this armor.

items: You ignore the armor’s Strength requirement.
You can don or doff the armor as a {@action Utilize} action. The armor can't be removed against your will.
You can use the Arcane Armor as a Spellcasting Focus for your Artificer spells.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Armor Model
entries: When you don your arcane armor, choose one of the following armor models: Guardian or Infiltrator. The model you choose gives you special benefits while you wear it.

entries.name: Guardian
entries: {@b Thunder Gauntlets.} You can use Intelligence instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes. You can roll {@damage 1d8} in place of their normal damage and you can deal thunder or bludgeoning damage (your choice) when you deal damage with your unarmed strikes. Also, a creature you hit has disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you until the start of your next turn.

{@b Defensive Field.} As a bonus action, you can gain temporary hit points equal to your Artificer level. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Infiltrator
entries: {@b Lightning Launcher.} A gemlike node appears on one of your armored fists or on the chest. It is a simple ranged weapon (range 100/300) that uses your Intelligence for attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals {@damage 2d6} lightning damage and applies the Vex mastery. Once every turn, on a hit you can deal {@damage 1d6} extra lightning damage.
{@b Powered Steps.} Your walking speed increases by 10 feet and you gain advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) ability checks.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Armor Modifications
entries: Your Arcane Armor counts as separate items for the purposes of your Infuse Items feature: armor, boots, helmet and weapon. Each of those items can bear one of your infusions, and the infusions transfer over if you change your armor's model. Moreover, the number of items you can infuse increases by 2, but those extra items must be part of your Arcane Armor.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Perfected Armor
entries: Your Arcane Armor gains additional benefits based on its model, as shown below.

entries.name: Guardian
entries: When a Huge or smaller creature you can see ends its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to pull it up to 25 feet directly to an unoccupied space. If you pull the target to a space within 5 feet of you, you can make a melee weapon attack against it as part of this reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tools of the Trade
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item calligrapher's supplies|xphb} and {@item cartographer's tools|xphb}.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Cartographer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Cartographer Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

rows: {@spell Faerie Fire|xphb}, {@spell Guiding Bolt|xphb}, {@spell Healing Word|xphb}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Adventurer's Atlas
entries: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can create a set of magical maps by touching a number of creatures equal to 1 plus your Intelligence modifier. Each target receives a magical map that lasts until you die or until you use this feature again. While carrying the map, a creature gains the following benefits.

items.name: Awareness
entries: The creature adds {@dice 1d4} to its Initiative rolls.

items.name: Positioning
entries: The creature knows the location of all other map holders that are on the same plane of existence as itself. When casting a spell or creating an effect that requires seeing the target, a map holder can target another map holder regardless of sight or cover, so long as it is still within the effect's range.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Mapping Magic
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items.name: Illuminated Cartography
entries: You can cast {@spell Guiding Bolt|xphb} without expending a spell slot. You can do so a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

items.name: Portal Jump
entries: On your turn, you can spend an amount of movement equal to half your Speed (round down) to teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of yourself or within 5 feet of a creature that is within 30 feet of you and holding one of your Adventurer's Atlas maps. You can't use this benefit if your Speed is 0.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Guided Precision
entries: Once per turn, whenever you deal damage with a spell from your Cartographer Spells list, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Ingenious Movement
entries: When you use your Flash of Genius, you or a willing creature of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of yourself can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see as part of that same Reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Superior Atlas
entries: Your Adventurer's Atlas improves, gaining the following benefits.

items.name: Safe Haven
entries: When a map holder would be reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, that creature can destroy its map. The creature's hit points instead change to a number equal to twice your Artificer level, and the creature is teleported to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you or another map holder of its choice.

items.name: Unerring Path
entries: You can cast {@spell Find the Path|xphb} without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without needing spell components. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item smith's tools|xphb} or another type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Forge Adept Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Forge Adept Spells table. These spells count as artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of artificer spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Soul Weapon
entries: You learn to imbue a weapon with a fraction of your spirit. When you finish a Long Rest, you can touch a weapon and make it your Soul Weapon, thus gaining the following features.

items: You can use its Weapon Mastery property.
It gains a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls, unless it already grants a higher bonus. This bonus increases to +2 at Artificer level 9 and increases to +3 at level 14.
The weapon loses its attributes if you use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Battle Ready
entries: Your combat training and your experiments with magic have paid off in two ways:

items: You gain proficiency with martial weapons.
When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity modifier, for the attack and damage rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Runes of War
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can create an aura in a 30-foot emanation that lasts for 10 minutes. The attacks of each ally in the emanation deal {@damage 1d4} extra Psychic damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and you regain all expended uses after a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Perfect Weapon
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action, you can transfer some of your Soul Weapon’s bonus to your AC. For every 1 you reduce your attack and damage rolls by (maximum of 3), you gain +1 to your AC. This effect lasts until the start of your next turn. Also, when you finish a Long rest, you gain one of the following benefits until the end of your next long rest.

items: You have resistance to Psychic damage, and you can’t be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.
When you hit with your Soul Weapon the target takes {@damage 1d6} extra Psychic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Bonus Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency in one skill or one tool of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Arcane Breakthrough
entries: You gain amazing insights into spell effects beyond the reach of most artificers. Choose one of the following classes: Cleric, Druid or Wizard. This class’s spells become part of your Breakthrough List. You can choose another class to add to your Breakthrough List at level 5 and level 9. When you prepare your spells, you can prepare one additional spell for each level and those spells must be chosen from your Breakthrough List. You can cast each of those spells once without expending a spell slot and regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest. The spells of your Breakthrough List are considered to be Artificer spells for you, and Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for them.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Cantrip Specialist
entries: You studied the basics of spellcasting more than most, gaining the following benefits.

items: You learn one cantrip of your choice from your Breakthrough List. At the end of a Long rest, you can change that cantrip with another from your Breakthrough List.
When you deal damage with a cantrip, you can roll one of its damage dice again and add it to the damage total.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Eccentric Versatility
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can cast one spell of your choice. This spell must be from your Breakthrough List and require an action to be cast. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Superior Breakthrough
entries: When you finish a Long rest, choose a spell from your Breakthrough List of a level equal to half your Artificer level (rounded up) up to level 7. You can cast the chosen spell once without expending a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Final Breakthrough
entries: When you cast one of your Artificer or Breakthrough spells, you can do so as if the spell was cast using a spell slot two levels higher than the spell slot you expend. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once) and regain all expended uses after you finish a Long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item woodcarver's tools|xphb}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Artillerist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Artillerist Spells table. These spells count as Artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Artificer spells you prepare.

entries.caption: Artillerist Spells
rows: {@spell Cone of Cold|xphb}, {@spell Wall of Force|xphb}

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Eldritch Cannon
entries: You learn how to create a magical cannon. Using {@item woodcarver's tools|xphb} or {@item smith's tools|xphb}, you can take an action to magically create a Small or Tiny eldritch cannon in an unoccupied space on a horizontal surface within 5 feet of you. A Small cannon occupies its space; a Tiny one can be held in one hand.
Once you create a cannon, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest or expend a spell slot of level 1 or higher. You can have only one cannon at a time.

The cannon is a magical object with AC 18 and hit points equal to five times your Artificer level. It is immune to Poison and Psychic damage. If forced to make an ability check or saving throw, treat all its ability scores as 10. {@spell Mending|xphb} cast on it restores {@dice 2d6} hit points. It disappears after 1 hour or when reduced to 0 hit points. You can dismiss it early as a Magic action.
When you create the cannon, choose one of the following types:

items.name: Flamethrower
entries: The cannon exhales fire in a 15-foot Cone. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 2d8} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. The fire ignites flammable objects not being worn or carried.

items.name: Force Ballista
entries: Make a ranged spell attack originating from the cannon at one creature or object within 120 feet. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Force damage and is pushed up to 5 feet away from the cannon.

items.name: Protector
entries: The cannon emits positive energy, granting itself and each creature of your choice within 10 feet a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).

On each of your turns, you can take a Bonus Action to activate the cannon if you are within 60 feet of it. As part of the same Bonus Action, you can command it to walk or climb up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space, if it has legs.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Arcane Firearm
entries: You know how to turn a wand, staff, or rod into an arcane firearm, a conduit for your destructive spells. When you finish a Long Rest, you can use {@item woodcarver's tools|xphb} to carve special sigils into such an item, turning it into your arcane firearm. The sigils disappear if you later carve them on a different item; otherwise they last indefinitely.
You can use your arcane firearm as a Spellcasting Focus. When you cast an Artificer spell through it, roll a {@dice d8} and add the result as a bonus to one of the spell's damage rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Explosive Cannon
entries: Every eldritch cannon you create is more destructive:

items: The cannon's damage rolls all increase by {@dice 1d8}.

As a Magic action, you can detonate the cannon if you are within 60 feet of it. Doing so destroys the cannon and forces each creature within 20 feet of it to make a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC, taking {@damage 3d8} Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Fortified Position
entries: You are a master at forming well-defended emplacements using your Eldritch Cannon:

items: You and your allies have {@variantrule Half Cover|xphb} while within 10 feet of a cannon you created with Eldritch Cannon, due to a shimmering field of magical protection the cannon emits.
You can now have two cannons at a time. You can create two with the same action (but not the same spell slot), and activate both with the same Bonus Action. The cannons can be identical or different.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item smith's tools|xphb}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of artisan's tools of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Battle Smith Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Battle Smith Spells table. These spells count as Artificer spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Artificer spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Battle Ready
entries: Your combat training and magical experiments have paid off in two ways:

items: You gain proficiency with martial weapons.
When you attack with a magic weapon, you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of Strength or Dexterity for the attack and damage rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Level 3: Steel Defender
entries: Your tinkering has borne you a faithful mechanical companion: a steel defender. It is friendly to you and your companions and obeys your commands.

In combat, the defender acts on your Initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its Reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the Dodge action, unless you take a Bonus Action to command it to take another action from its stat block or another action of your choice.
If {@spell Mending|xphb} is cast on it, it regains {@dice 2d6} hit points. It disappears if reduced to 0 hit points, if you die, or if you dismiss it as a Magic action.
At the end of a Long Rest, you can create a new steel defender if you have {@item smith's tools|xphb} with you. If you already have one, the previous one disappears immediately.
You can restore the defender to its hit point maximum by expending one of your Artificer Infusions on it.

subclassFeature.name: Level 5: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, rather than once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Level 9: Arcane Jolt
entries: You have learned new ways to channel arcane energy to harm or heal. When you hit a target with a magic weapon attack, or your steel defender hits a target, you can channel magical energy through the strike to create one of the following effects:

items: The target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} Force damage.
Choose one creature or object you can see within 30 feet of the target. Magical energy leaps from the target to it, restoring {@dice 2d6} hit points.

entries: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum once), but no more than once per turn. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Level 15: Improved Defender
entries: Your Arcane Jolt and steel defender become more powerful:

items: The extra damage and healing of Arcane Jolt both increase to {@damage 4d6}.
Your steel defender gains a +2 bonus to Armor Class.
Whenever your steel defender uses its Deflect Attack, the attacker takes Force damage equal to {@damage 1d4} + your Intelligence modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Artificer Goggles
entries: {@i Prerequisite: a pair of goggles}
These goggles have 4 charges. The wearer can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Darkvision|xphb} or {@spell Detect Magic|xphb} spell. The goggles regain {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Bane Weapon
entries: {@i Prerequisites: Level 6+; A simple or martial weapon (requires attunement)}

The weapon has 4 charges. This weapon grants a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls made with it. When you infuse this item, choose one of these creature types: aberration, dragon, fiend or undead. When the wielder hits a creature of this type with the weapon, they can expend 1 charge to deal {@damage 2d6} extra force damage. The weapon's bonus increases to +2 when you reach Artificer level 10. The weapon regains {@dice 1d4} charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Blasting Focus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Alchemist, Artillerist or Maverick subclasses (requires attunement)}
This focus has 4 charges. Once every turn, when you cast an Artificer spell that deals cold, fire or lightning, you can expend 1 charge to gain one of the following benefits.
One roll of the spell deals extra damage equal to your Artificer level.
The spell ignores damage resistances.
The focus regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Bracer of Magical Strength
entries: {@i Prerequisite: A bracelet or similar garment (requires attunement)}
This bracer has 6 charges that can be expended in the following ways:
When the wearer makes a Strength check or a Strength saving throw, it can expend 1 charge to add a bonus to the roll equal to your Intelligence modifier.
When the wearer attempts a grapple or shove, it can expend 1 charge to make so the DC is based on its Intelligence instead of its Strength.
The bracer regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Chameleon Cloak
entries: {@i Prerequisite: a cloak or mantle}

This cloak has 6 charges. The wearer can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Disguise Self|xphb} spell. From Artificer level 6, they also can expend 1 charge to cast the {@spell Alter Self|xphb} spell.
The cloak regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Cloaking Field
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 9+, Armorer (Infiltrator) subclass (requires attunement)}

A sophisticated device allows you to bolster your Dampening Field in order to camouflage with the environment. At the start of your turn, you can become {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn, or until you make an attack or cast a spell. While invisible in this way, your speed is reduced to 10 feet unless it was already lower.

optionalfeature.name: Fortify Defense
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 6+, a suit of armor}

The armor has 4 charges. A creature gains a +1 bonus to Armor Class while wearing this armor. As a reaction, when the wearer suffers a critical hit, they can expend 1 charge and count that hit as a normal hit instead. Any effects triggered by the critical hit are negated.
The armor regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Healing Salve
entries: {@i Prerequisite: A jar of lotion}

A creature can apply this magical salve to heal wounds. The healing salve has 4 charges. As a bonus action, a creature holding it can expend one charge and touch a creature, healing it a number of hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Intelligence modifier.
The salve regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

optionalfeature.name: Hydraulics Diverter
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 9+, Armorer (Guardian) subclass (requires attunement)}

An intricate system allows you to divert magical power from your defenses to your Thunder Gauntlets. Once per turn, when you hit an enemy with an unarmed strike, you can increase the damage dealt by a number of d8s up to your Proficiency Bonus. Until the start of your next turn, your Armor Class is lowered by the same number.

optionalfeature.name: Quiver of Energy
entries: {@i Prerequisite: a quiver}

When you infuse this item, choose one damage type: cold, fire, or lightning. Once every turn, when the bearer hits a target with an attack using an ammunition from this quiver, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage of the chosen type. The extra damage increases to {@damage 1d10} when you reach Artificer level 6 and {@damage 1d12} when you reach Artificer level 10.

optionalfeature.name: Radiant Weapon
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 6+, a weapon (requires attunement)}

The weapon has 4 charges. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made using the weapon, also on a hit it deals {@damage 1d4} extra radiant damage. As a reaction immediately after being hit by an attack, the wielder can expend 1 charge and the attacker must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or become {@condition Blinded} until the end of its next turn. These bonuses increase to +2 and {@damage 2d4} extra Radiant damage when you reach Artificer level 10. The weapon regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

spell.name: The Leucotype

components.m: a silver-coated copper sheet worth 10+ gp which the spell consumes

entries: Holding the copper sheet used as a component you can magically imprint a perfect image of the scenario before you at the moment of the casting. If you are experiencing hallucinations, perceiving illusions, or otherwise seeing things that are not physically present, those visual phenomena are also captured on the plate exactly as you perceive them.

{@b Casting as a Circle Spell.} Instead of a still image, you imprint on the copper sheet a scene happening before you that includes moving images and sounds. When you cast this spell you choose a command word that magically activates the recorded scene once used within 5 feet of the sheet. The recorded scene can have a duration up to 10 seconds per secondary caster.

spell.name: Calantha's Solar Gravity
entries: At your command gravity briefly intensifies, pulling creatures inward before erupting with a burst of radiant solar force. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you can see within range must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is pulled 10 feet toward the center of the area and takes {@damage 8d6} radiant damage. A creature pulled by this effect is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Nyx's Animate Dead
entries: You animate dead creatures that appear as a Large horde of intangible corpses or spirits in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The horde lasts for the duration and you choose the dead’s form, such as ghosts or skeletons. When you move on your turn, you can also move the horde up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Whenever the horde moves within 5 feet of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters a space within 5 feet of the horde or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage and is {@condition restrained} while it remains within 5 feet of the horde. On a successful save, it only takes half that damage. The horde can restrain only one creature at a time and a restrained creature can repeat its saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Nyx's Conjure Barrage

components.m: a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP

entries: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and conjure similar spectral weapons (or ammunition appropriate to the weapon) that launch forward and then disappear. Each creature of your choice that you can see in a 60-foot Cone makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d10} force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Nyx's Conjure Volley

components.m: a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP

entries: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and choose a point within range. Hundreds of similar spectral weapons (or ammunition appropriate to the weapon) fall in a volley and then disappear. Each creature of your choice that you can see in a 40-foot-radius, 20-foot-high Cylinder centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 8d10} force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

spell.name: Elmina's Unleash Potential

components.m: magical ink worth 600 GP or the very page in your spellbook, if you have one, where this spell was written that the spell consumes

entries: You continuously channel your magical power within others to express their inner potential and make them shine enough to be front page material: what a scoop! When you cast this spell and at the start of your following turns, you can target up to two willing creatures within range and grant each one of the following effects. If an effect shares the name of a spell, those creatures are considered to be under the effect of a spell with the same name.

items: {@b Protection from Energy.} The creature has Resistance to one damage type of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning or Thunder.
{@b Far Step.} As a Bonus Action, the target can teleport 60 feet in a space it can see.
{@b Magnify Senses.} The target has Blindsight out of a range of 30 feet.
{@b Bless.} When the target makes an attack roll or a saving throw, it adds {@dice 1d4} to it.
{@b Fount of Moonlight.} The target’s melee attacks deal {@damage 2d6} extra Radiant damage.
{@b Greater Invisibility.} The target has the {@condition invisible} condition.
{@b Unfeeling.} The target gains 10 temporary Hit Points, damage can’t break their concentration if part of it removes these temporary Hit Points.
{@b Haste.} The target speed is doubled and it gains an additional action that can be used to take only the Attack (one attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Utilize action.

entries: The effect lasts until the start of your next turn. After the spell ends, you’re incapable of casting any spell by using a spell slot until you finish a short or long rest.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: When cast at a higher level, you can target an additional creature per spell level above 6.

spell.name: Nyx's Ensnaring Strike

time.condition: which you take immediately after hitting a target with a Melee or Ranged weapon

entries: As you hit the target, grasping vines appear on it. The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} piercing damage and makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition restrained} condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the vines shrivel away, and the spell ends. While Restrained, the target takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC ending the spell on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage for both effects of the spell increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Forge's Codex of Delirium
entries: A creature you can see within range is thrown in an hallucination that seamlessly overtakes its reality and must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save it experiences an entire hallucinatory day in a single day, you have full control over what it experiences, and you can describe the nature of the day that it lives (choosing to make it as wonderful or nightmarish as you please). The illusion it experiences is totally indistinguishable from reality. You know everything that the target experiences in this hallucination, for example, you know if it confesses to any horrible crimes or if it reveals its weaknesses or fears. At the end of the hallucination, the target gains one level of exhaustion and you can deal {@damage 12d8} psychic damage to it. On a successful save, it breaks free from the illusion and you can deal it only half that damage.

spell.name: Nyx's Hail of Thorns

time.condition: which you take immediately after hitting a target with a Melee or Ranged weapon

entries: As you hit the creature, this spell creates a rain of thorns that sprouts from your weapon. The target of the attack and each creature within 10 feet of it make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Lightning Strike

time.condition: which you take immediately after hitting a target with a Melee or Ranged weapon

entries: As you hit, your weapon blasts in a lightning bolt strike. The target takes an extra {@damage 3d8} lightning damage from the attack. In addition, each creature of your choice within 10 feet of the target then makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage for both effects of the spell increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Randarc's Chains of Pure Hatred

time.condition: which you take immediately after hitting a target with a Melee weapon or Unarmed Strike

entries: As you hit the target you focus your hatred towards the enemies and their spells, to magically evoke chains that will bind them. The target takes an extra {@damage 3d6} force damage and makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition restrained} condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the chain breaks, and the spell ends.

Whenever the restrained creature casts a spell, it makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.
The target can take an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC ending the spell on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 4.

spell.name: Summon Homunculus
entries: You summon a special homunculus in an unoccupied space within range. This creature uses the Homunculus Servant stat block. If you already have a homunculus from this spell, the homunculus is replaced by the new one. You determine the homunculus's appearance, such as a mechanical-looking bird, winged vial, or miniature animate cauldron. The homunculus is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Nyx's Zephyr’s Strike

time.condition: which you take immediately after hitting a target with a Melee or Ranged weapon

entries: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} force damage from the attack. Until the end of your turn, your speed increases by 15 feet and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

race.name: Warforged
entries: The first warforged were creations of wood and stone, golems animated by mighty druidic rituals. After centuries these secret rituals were found by spellcasters far removed from the first druids who animated golems gifted with free will. Now these rituals are practiced in two places: Lantan and Thay. Lantan's warforged are created by the church of Gond through its unique magic, like many Lantan inventions they are strange and frightening to the rest of Faerun. The warforged of Thay appear to be simpler beings, often aimed only at battle or assassination. Some Thayan Zulkir have studied ancient rituals that combine necromancy and transmutation to provide superior intelligence to their grotesque magical constructs.

The shape of all warforged is human-like, but optimized to perform a specific task: fighting, traveling or protecting. The materials and appearance of a warforged depend on the technique used by its creator. Druidic warforged are human-like wooden golems, sometimes covered with stone plates. Those of Lantan are constructs covered in metal above a wooden body. Finally, Thay warforged can be made with organic components, built from a human skeleton or to appear more human in order to spy and assassinate.

entries.name: Constructed Resilience
entries: You have resistance to Poison damage. You also have Advantage on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition poisoned} condition.

entries.name: Sentry's Rest
entries: You don't need to sleep, and magic can't put you to sleep. You can finish a Long Rest in 6 hours that you must spend inactive, but not {@condition unconscious}.

entries.name: Specialized Lineage
entries: You've been designed for a particular work. Choose a design from the Specialized Design table. You gain the level 1 benefit of that design. When you reach character levels 3, you gain an additional benefit that you can use a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus and regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

entries.caption: Specialized Design
rows: You have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks.
When you hit with a weapon attack, you can deal {@damage 1d8} extra force damage to the target of the attack.
While wearing armor, you gain a +1 on AC.
At the start of your turn, you can give yourself temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.
Your movement speed is increased by 10 feet.
You can take the Disengage action as a bonus action.

feat.name: Crafter (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Tool Proficiency.} You gain proficiency with three different Artisan’s Tools of your choice from the Fast Crafting table.
{@b Discount.} Whenever you craft a common or uncommon magic item, you receive a 25 percent discount on the crafting’s cost.

{@b Fast Crafting.} When you finish a Long rest, you can craft one piece of gear from the Fast Crafting table, provided you have proficiency with the Artisan’s Tools associated with it. The item lasts until you finish another Long rest, at which point it falls apart.

feat.name: Harper Agent (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Distracting Melody.} When you take the Help action to assist an ally's attack roll, the enemy you're distracting can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, provided the enemy can see or hear you.
{@b Reactive Teamwork.} Once during your turn, immediately after you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can take the Help action as part of the attack.
{@b Thieves’ Cant.} You know {@language thieves' cant}.

feat.name: Savage Attacker (TAF)
entries: You’re trained to deal particularly damaging strikes. Once every turn, when you deal damage with a weapon or unarmed strike, you can deal {@damage 1d4} extra damage. This damage increases when you reach level 5 to {@damage 1d6}, level 11 to {@damage 1d8} and level 17 to {@damage 1d10}.

feat.name: Adept of Black Magic (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Ambitious Magic.} You learn a level 1 spell of your choice from the Enchantment or Necromancy school. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long rest before you can cast it in this way again. Your spellcasting ability is the one increased by this feat.

{@b Life Channel.} When a creature fails the saving throw against one of your spells that deals damage, you can expend and roll a number of hit dice up to the spell’s level and increase one damage roll of the spell by the total rolled.

feat.name: Adept of Red Magic (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Insightful Magic.} You learn a level 1 spell of your choice from the Illusion or Transmutation school. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long rest before you can cast it in this way again. Your spellcasting ability is the one increased by this feat.

{@b Balance Fate.} When you roll a 9 or lower on an attack roll or ability check, you can treat the roll as a 10 instead. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long rest unless you expend a level 1+ spell slot to restore the use of it.

feat.name: Adept of White Magic (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Protective Magic.} You learn a level 1 spell of your choice from the abjuration or divination school. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. The spell’s spellcasting ability is the one you increased with this feat.

{@b Protective Ward.} When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can take a Reaction to expend a spell slot and magically protect it. Roll a number of d6s equal to the spell slot’s level and reduce the damage by the total rolled plus your Proficiency Bonus.

feat.name: Agent of Order (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Stasis Strike.} Once every turn, when you deal damage with an attack, you can deal {@damage 1d6} extra Force damage. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target is {@condition restrained} until the end of its next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Baleful Scion (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Drink of Avarice.} Once every turn, when you deal damage with an attack, you can deal {@damage 1d6} extra Necrotic damage. You regain a number of hit points equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Bomber (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Bomb Maker.} When you finish a Long rest, you create a number of Acid, Alchemist’s Fire or Holy Water equal to your Proficiency Bonus. These items last until you finish another Long Rest, at which point they fall apart.
{@b Empowered Reagents.} You can add the modifier of the ability score increased by this feat to the damage you deal throwing a vial or flask.
{@b Far Lobber.} When you throw a vial or flask, you can target an object or creature you can see within 40 feet of yourself.

feat.name: Cartomancer (TAF)
entries: You have learned the magical secrets of cards. You gain the following benefit.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Card Tricks.} You learn {@spell Prestidigitation|xphb} and can conceal the verbal and somatic components of the spell as ordinary conversation or card handling.

{@b Hidden Ace.} At the end of a Long rest, choose a spell from the spell list of your class and imbue it in a card. The chosen spell must have a casting time of Action, and must be a spell you can cast with the level you have in that class. While the card is imbued, you can use a Bonus Action to cast the spell without verbal or somatic components. The card loses its magic after 8 hours or after you cast the spell.

feat.name: Chromatic Dragon’s Gift (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Chromatic Infusion.} As a Bonus Action, you can infuse a weapon or unarmed strike with one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning or Poison. For 1 minute, the weapon or unarmed strike deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the chosen type. Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long rest.

{@b Reactive Resistance.} When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning or Poison damage, you can take a Reaction to give you resistance to that instance of damage. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Ember of the Fire Giant (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Born of Flame.} You gain resistance to Fire damage.

{@b Searing Ignition.} Once every turn, you can replace an attack to force each creature of your choice within a 15-foot emanation to make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, it takes {@damage 2d10} Fire damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, it only takes half as much damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Fury of the Frost Giant (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength, Constitution or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Born of Ice.} You gain resistance to Cold damage.

{@b Frigid Retribution.} When you take damage from a creature you can see within 30 feet, you can take a Reaction and force it to make a Constitution saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, it takes {@damage 2d8} Cold damage, and its speed is 0 until the end of its next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Guile of the Cloud Giant (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Cloudy Escape.} When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to give yourself resistance to that instance damage and teleport 30 feet in an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Gunner (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Dexterity or Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Deadly Shot.} Once every turn, when you hit with a Pistol or Musket, you can deal extra damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus.
{@b Gun Expertise.} You gain proficiency in Pistols and Muskets, you ignore their Loading property and can load a piece of ammunition even if you lack a free hand.
{@b Keen Eye.} Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls with Muskets and Pistols.

feat.name: Keenness of the Stone Giant (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Cavernous Sight.} You gain Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

{@b Stone Throw.} As a Bonus Action, make a ranged attack (60/90) using the ability score you increased with this feat for attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals Force damage equal to {@damage 1d10}, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus) or be knocked {@condition prone}. You can use this Bonus Action a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Metallic Dragon’s Gift (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Metallic Healing.} As a Bonus Action, roll a number of d6s equal to your proficiency bonus. A creature you touch regains a number of hit points equal to the total rolled. Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long rest.

{@b Protective Wings.} When a creature you can see within 10 feet of you is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to protect it with spectral wings. You grant a bonus to its AC equal to your Proficiency Bonus against that attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Knight of the Crown (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Commanding Rally.} As a Bonus Action, you can command an ally within 30 feet of you. The target can use a reaction to make a weapon attack and if the attack hits, it deals {@damage 1d10} extra damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Knight of the Sword (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Frightful Strike.} When you hit with an attack, you can deal {@damage 1d6} extra Psychic damage and force the target to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, it is {@condition frightened} until the end of its next turn. On a success, it has disadvantage on its next attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Poisoner (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Potent Poison.} You gain proficiency with the {@item poisoner's kit|xphb}. Also, poisons used by you ignore resistance to Poison damage and their DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus.

{@b Brew Poisons.} When you finish a Long rest, you create a number of poison vials equal to your Proficiency Bonus. Once applied, the poison lasts for 10 minutes or until you deal damage with the poisoned item. When you deal damage with the poisoned item, you deal {@damage 2d8} Poison damage and the target of the attack must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn. The vials last until you finish another Long rest, at which point it falls apart.

feat.name: Righteous Heritor (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Soothe Pain.} When a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use a reaction to reduce the damage by {@dice 1d10} + your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

feat.name: Soul of the Storm Giant (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Born in the Storm.} You gain resistance to Lightning damage.

{@b Maelstrom Strike.} Once every turn, when you hit a creature with an attack, you can summon storm winds around it. The target must succeed in a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 plus your modifier of the ability score increased by this feat and your Proficiency Bonus) or have disadvantage on attack rolls and have its speed halved until the end of its next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long rest.

feat.name: Street Justice (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity by 1, to a maximum of 20.
{@b Headlock.} Your allies have advantage on attack rolls against creatures {@condition grappled} by you.
{@b Quick Lock.} When you take the Attack Action, you can replace one of your attacks with a use of Chain, Manacles or Rope.
{@b Sturdy Knot.} When you use Chain, Manacles or Rope on a creature, add your Proficiency Bonus to the DC to escape or burst out.

feat.name: Boon of Immortality (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase one ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 30.
{@b Everlasting.} You stop aging, are immune to any effect that would age you and you can't die from old age. Moreover you don't need to eat, drink, or breathe to survive.
{@b Unbreakable.} When you suffer damage, you can take Reaction to gain resistance to that damage type until the start of your next turn.
{@b Undying.} You have advantage on Death Saving Throws.

feat.name: Boon of Spell Invention (TAF)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma by 1, to a maximum of 30.

{@b Create Spell.} You can mimic the archmages of old by inventing a spell never seen before. To create the spell, you must study for 10 days and expend 100 gp for each level of the new spell in order to buy magical components needed for the creation.
{@b Signature Spell.} Once your new spell is created, you always have it prepared and it doesn’t count against the number of your prepared spells.
